args.query: SELECT id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c FROM casehistory__c LIMIT 50;

exec_args.sql: SELECT id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c FROM casehistory__c LIMIT 50;

args.query:
SELECT c.id AS case_id, c.createddate, c.closeddate, MIN(ch.newvalue__c) AS ownerid, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate::timestamp - c.createddate::timestamp)) AS handle_seconds, COUNT(ch.*) AS owner_assignment_count
FROM "Case" c
JOIN casehistory__c ch ON ch.caseid__c = c.id AND ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
WHERE c.closeddate >= '2023-05-02' AND c.closeddate <= '2023-09-02'
GROUP BY c.id, c.createddate, c.closeddate
HAVING COUNT(ch.*) = 1;

exec_args.sql:
SELECT c.id AS case_id, c.createddate, c.closeddate, MIN(ch.newvalue__c) AS ownerid, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate::timestamp - c.createddate::timestamp)) AS handle_seconds, COUNT(ch.*) AS owner_assignment_count
FROM "Case" c
JOIN casehistory__c ch ON ch.caseid__c = c.id AND ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
WHERE c.closeddate >= '2023-05-02' AND c.closeddate <= '2023-09-02'
GROUP BY c.id, c.createddate, c.closeddate
HAVING COUNT(ch.*) = 1;

args.query:
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT id, createddate, closeddate FROM "Case" WHERE closeddate >= '2023-05-02' AND closeddate <= '2023-09-02'
),
owner_assignments AS (
  SELECT ch.caseid__c as case_id, ch.newvalue__c as ownerid
  FROM casehistory__c ch
  JOIN closed_cases cc ON ch.caseid__c = cc.id
  WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment' AND ch.newvalue__c IS NOT NULL
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ownerid, COUNT(DISTINCT case_id) AS assign_case_count
  FROM owner_assignments
  GROUP BY ownerid
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT cc.id as case_id, cc.createddate, cc.closeddate, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cc.closeddate::timestamp - cc.createddate::timestamp)) AS handle_seconds, oa.ownerid
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN (
    SELECT case_id, MIN(ownerid) as ownerid, COUNT(*) as cnt
    FROM owner_assignments
    GROUP BY case_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
  ) oa ON oa.case_id = cc.id
)
SELECT nt.ownerid, AVG(nt.handle_seconds) as avg_handle, COUNT(*) as num_cases
FROM non_transferred_cases nt
JOIN assign_counts ac ON ac.ownerid = nt.ownerid
WHERE ac.assign_case_count > 1
GROUP BY nt.ownerid
ORDER BY avg_handle ASC
LIMIT 1;

exec_args.sql:
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT id, createddate, closeddate FROM "Case" WHERE closeddate >= '2023-05-02' AND closeddate <= '2023-09-02'
),
owner_assignments AS (
  SELECT ch.caseid__c as case_id, ch.newvalue__c as ownerid
  FROM casehistory__c ch
  JOIN closed_cases cc ON ch.caseid__c = cc.id
  WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment' AND ch.newvalue__c IS NOT NULL
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ownerid, COUNT(DISTINCT case_id) AS assign_case_count
  FROM owner_assignments
  GROUP BY ownerid
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT cc.id as case_id, cc.createddate, cc.closeddate, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cc.closeddate::timestamp - cc.createddate::timestamp)) AS handle_seconds, oa.ownerid
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN (
    SELECT case_id, MIN(ownerid) as ownerid, COUNT(*) as cnt
    FROM owner_assignments
    GROUP BY case_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
  ) oa ON oa.case_id = cc.id
)
SELECT nt.ownerid, AVG(nt.handle_seconds) as avg_handle, COUNT(*) as num_cases
FROM non_transferred_cases nt
JOIN assign_counts ac ON ac.ownerid = nt.ownerid
WHERE ac.assign_case_count > 1
GROUP BY nt.ownerid
ORDER BY avg_handle ASC
LIMIT 1;

args.query:
SELECT ch.newvalue__c AS ownerid, COUNT(DISTINCT ch.caseid__c) AS cases_assigned
FROM casehistory__c ch
JOIN "Case" c ON ch.caseid__c = c.id
WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment' AND c.closeddate >= '2023-05-02' AND c.closeddate <= '2023-09-02' AND ch.newvalue__c IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY ch.newvalue__c
ORDER BY cases_assigned DESC;

exec_args.sql:
SELECT ch.newvalue__c AS ownerid, COUNT(DISTINCT ch.caseid__c) AS cases_assigned
FROM casehistory__c ch
JOIN "Case" c ON ch.caseid__c = c.id
WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment' AND c.closeddate >= '2023-05-02' AND c.closeddate <= '2023-09-02' AND ch.newvalue__c IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY ch.newvalue__c
ORDER BY cases_assigned DESC;
